title: Bridge Networks, DNS, Ports, Routing, IPv6, and Exposure Policy · Docker Build Room
summary: A platform pairing session must produce the first working least-exposure container network topology for bridge networks, dns, ports, routing, ipv6, and exposure policy, with predictions recorded before each simulated build or lifecycle change. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of least-exposure container network topology while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defen…
objectives: Trace container interfaces, network namespaces, veth pairs, bridge networks, routes, NAT, firewall rules, and platform boundaries.
Use user-defined networks, embedded DNS, service names, aliases, attach/detach behavior, and IP churn without hard-coded addresses.
Distinguish listening, EXPOSE, expose, publish, host address, host port, container port, and direct-routing behavior.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# docker-basics cumulative container delivery portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never connect to a Docker daemon, mount a socket, or execute learner commands.
workspace: docker
version-baseline:
  engine: 29.6.1
  compose: 5.3.1
  buildx: 0.35.0
  buildkit: 0.31.1
  dockerfile-frontend: 1.25
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-environment
evidence:

steps.title: Commit to the mission model · Workshop Docker Network Namespace Veth Bridge
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop Compose stack publishes its database on every host interface, pins a container IP, and calls localhost from the API. Split trust networks, use service DNS, restrict publishing, and verify ingress separately from egress.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Commit to the mission model · Workshop Docker Network Namespace Veth Bridge.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for trace container interfaces, network namespaces, veth pairs, bridge networks, routes, nat, firewall rules, and platform boundaries. For Commit to the mission model · Workshop Docker Network Namespace Veth Bridge, keep Docker Network Namespace Veth Bridge connected to mechanically reviewable Docker artifact and transfer evidence.

content.text: A workshop Compose stack publishes its database on every host interface, pins a container IP, and calls localhost from the API. Split trust networks, use service DNS, restrict publishing, and verify ingress separately from egress.

content.title: Commit before feedback
content.text: Choose from the stated data before opening the worked model. Record which Docker Network Namespace Veth Bridge condition decided your prediction.

content.label: Authority to consult after committing
content.value: Docker Networking and Port Publishing Documentation — Controls bridge networks, DNS, aliases, routes, NAT, gateway modes, IPv6, port publishing, packet filtering, segmentation, and platform differences.

hints: For Commit to the mission model · Workshop Docker Network Namespace Veth Bridge, name the daemon authority, immutable identity, process, storage, network, secret, and cleanup boundary, Docker Network Namespace Veth Bridge precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Commit to the mission model · Workshop Docker Network Namespace Veth Bridge, trace this concrete input and predict build, image, container, mount, network, Compose, policy, and teardown evidence before reviewing it: A workshop Compose stack publishes its database on every host interface, pins a container IP, and calls localhost from the API. Split trust networks, use service DNS, restrict publishing, and verify ingress separately from egress.

For Commit to the mission model · Workshop Docker Network Namespace Veth Bridge, challenge the documented misconception with manifest, inspect, lifecycle, failure, repair, and transfer evidence: A Docker bridge is the same object and trust boundary as a physical Ethernet switch.

options.text: State the Docker Network Namespace Veth Bridge preconditions, preserve earlier contracts, and name the trace or changed-input result that will decide success.
A Docker bridge is the same object and trust boundary as a physical Ethernet switch. That claim fails the commit to the mission model evidence boundary for Docker Network Namespace Veth Bridge.

For commit to the mission model, copy one familiar Docker command, omit daemon authority, immutable identity, failure, cleanup, and disposable-environment transfer checks, then accept a successful build as production proof. This does not satisfy Docker Network Namespace Veth Bridge for least-exposure container network topology.

steps.title: Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Docker Ipv6 Host Platform Diagnostics
steps.instruction:
Append the Docker evidence item headed "  # Evidence: docker-ipv6-host-platform-diagnostics-1ng76f". Tailor its network-contract.yaml change to the current stakeholder case, retain a deterministic review command, reject one concrete failure, and record a repair. The browser analyzes text only; run Docker commands later in an authorized disposable transfer environment.

Evidence target: Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Docker Ipv6 Host Platform Diagnostics.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Docker Ipv6 Host Platform Diagnostics into observable performance evidence. For Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Docker Ipv6 Host Platform Diagnostics, keep Docker Ipv6 Host Platform Diagnostics connected to mechanically reviewable Docker artifact and transfer evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: docker-ipv6-host-platform-diagnostics-1ng76f
  - competency: docker-ipv6-host-platform-diagnostics
    artifact: network-contract.yaml
    decision: ipv6 host platform diagnostics
    scenario-variant: 2
    environment: IPv6-enabled Compose project on a shared CI runner
    fault-injection: move a tag while the immutable digest remains available
    retained-proof: timestamped lifecycle transition and bounded cleanup result
    case-key: 1ng76f
    change: |
      frontend-network: published-edge
      backend-network: internal
      service-discovery: service-name
      host-binding: 127.0.0.1:8080
      database-published: false
    verify: docker network inspect intake-backend --format json
    failure: Reject container IP pinning, localhost-as-peer assumptions, accidental all-interface publishing, or an exposed database port.
    repair: Use project-scoped DNS names, separate networks by trust, bind only the required host interface, and test egress and ingress independently.
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.
